title: Davallia thwaitesii
intro: Davallia thwaitesii, also known as the Thwaites' leather fern, is a species of fern in the family Davalliaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Davallia thwaitesii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Davallia thwaitesii is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is used to make remedies such as tonics, capsules, and teas, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Davallia thwaitesii is classified as a species of fern. It belongs to the genus Davallia, which is part of the family Davalliaceae. The Davallia thwaitesii is native to Sri Lanka and other parts of South Asia. This species is a type of tropical fern that typically grows in humid and subtropical regions. Its classification is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern.
